articles.headline: Portugal, Ronaldo frustrated in opening draw with Congo DR
articles.description: Congo DR produced another stunning result at the 2026 World Cup, as the African side held one of the pre-tournament favorites, Portugal, to a 1-1 draw in Houston on Wednesday.

images.name: Congo celeb vs Portugal [1296x729]
images.caption: Congo DR celebrate their equalizing goal against Portugal.
images.alt: Cedric Bakambu #17 of DR Congo leaps on top of teammates to celebrate a goal by Yoane Wissa #20 of DR Congo during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group K match between Portugal and Congo DR on June 17, 2026, at Houston Stadium in Houston, Texas.
images.credit: David Buono/Icon Sportswire via Getty Images

articles.headline: Noni Madueke, Jude Bellingham start for England vs. Croatia, Bukayo Saka on bench
articles.description: Noni Madueke, Jude Bellingham and Anthony Gordon will operate behind Harry Kane in England's World Cup opener against Croatia

images.name: Madueke Bellingham [1296x729]
images.alt: Noni Madueke #20 of England speaks to teammate Jude Bellingham during the International Friendly match between England and Costa Rica at Inter&Co Stadium on June 10, 2026 in Orlando, Florida.
images.credit: Eddie Keogh - The FA/The FA via Getty Images

articles.headline: World Cup Daily: Ronaldo, Portugal take on Congo DR
articles.description: It has officially been one week of 2026 FIFA World Cup action. Enjoy all the features, news, video analysis and color from around the competition.

images.name: Portugal team celeb v DR Congo 260617 [1296x1296]
images.credit: Julian Finney - FIFA/FIFA via Getty Images

articles.headline: USMNT's Aaronson, Robinson say Messi's hat-trick 'not normal'
articles.description: USMNT's Aaronson, Robinson say Messi's hat-trick 'not normal'

images.name: USMNT's Aaronson, Robinson say Messi's hat-trick 'not normal'
images.caption: USMNT's Aaronson, Robinson say Messi's hat-trick 'not normal'

articles.headline: South Africa's Themba Zwane handed three-match ban for red card against Mexico in FIFA World Cup opener
articles.description: South Africa attacking midfielder Themba Zwane has been suspended for three matches for his red card against Mexico, subject to appeal, by the FIFA Disciplinary Committee.

images.name: Mexico, South Africa red cards [1296x729]
images.caption: The three red cards in Mexico vs. South Africa is a record for the opening match of a World Cup.
images.credit: Yuri CORTEZ / AFP via Getty Images

images.name: Gomez on South Africa's red cards: 'I have no idea what got into them'
images.caption: Gomez on South Africa's red cards: 'I have no idea what got into them'.

articles.headline: All Herve Renard has to do with Tunisia is 'stop the bleeding' - Patrice Beaumelle
articles.description: Herve Renard's long-term assistant coach Patrice Beaumelle believes that the newly appointed Tunisia coach must prioritise the North Africans' disastrous defensive record before anything else at the FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Herve Renard Tunisia [1296x729]
images.caption: Herve Renard arrived to coach Tunisia by Tuesday.

images.name: herve Renard Saudi [1296x729]
images.caption: Herve Renard led Saudi Arabia to a shock win over Argentina at the 2022 World Cup. That Argentina went on to win the tournament is unimportant.

images.name: Renard: 'I did not hesitate for a second' taking Tunisia job
images.caption: Renard: 'I did not hesitate for a second' taking Tunisia job

article.headline: Marko Arnautovic helps Austria to first World Cup win in 36 years against Jordan
article.description: Austria won a World Cup game for the first time in 36 years with a 3-1 victory Tuesday over a Jordan team making their debut at the global showpiece.
article.linkText: Austria get first WC win in 36 years against Jordan

images.name: Austria  [594x334]
images.caption: Marko Arnautovic scores from the spot to help Austria to a 3-1 win

peers.name: Austria  [385x385]
peers.caption: Marko Arnautovic scores from the spot to help Austria to a 3-1 win

peers.caption: Marko Arnautovic scores from the spot to help Austria to a 3-1 win

peers.name: Austria  [594x238]
peers.caption: Marko Arnautovic scores from the spot to help Austria to a 3-1 win

article.story:
Austria won a World Cup game for the first time in 36 years with a 3-1 victory Tuesday over a Jordan team making their debut at the global showpiece.

An own goal from a corner kick that deflected off Jordanian defender Yazan Al Arab gave Austria a 2-1 lead in the 76th minute. Marko Arnautovic's penalty kick deep in stoppage team extended the margin.

The game between a Jordan team making their debut and an Austrian squad back on soccer's biggest stage after missing the last six tournaments featured plenty of excitement.

It peaked in a stretch in the second half that featured an equaliser by Jordan and two balls sent into the net off corner kicks by Austria --  with only one of those counting.

Substitute Arnautovic had appeared to give Austria the lead when he converted from a corner kick after a misplay by Jordanian goalkeeper Yazeed Abulaila. But the decision went to VAR and the referee called a hand ball on Stefan Posch whose arm touched the ball in the lead up.

Austria got another corner kick shortly after the second-half hydration break and the took a 2-1 lead on Al Arab's own goal.

That proved to be the difference for Austria, which earned its first win at the World Cup since beating the United States in a group-stage match in 1990. Jordan fell short in its attempt to become the first country to win its World Cup debut game since Senegal did it in 2002 against defending-champion France.

Romano Schmid scored in the 20th minute for Austria when he capped off an impressive buildup that put the Jordanian defense on its heels. Xaver Schlager fed a pass to Schmid, who hit a perfectly struck right-foot shot from the edge of the area into the top corner of the net.

Ali Olwan tied it for Jordan in the 50th when made a long run into the box on a counterattack and beat Alexander Schlager with a right-foot shot that delighted the large contingent of Jordanian fans.

Jordan will return to Santa Clara for its second match next Monday against Algeria, which lost 3-0 to Argentina in the first Group J match. Austria will face Lionel Messi and Argentina in the Dallas area that same day.

stats.description: The number of shots attempted.

stats.description: The number of through balls attempted.

stats.name: expectedGoals
stats.description: Total expected goals

stats.name: expectedAssists
stats.description: Total Expected Assists

stats.name: expectedAssistsOpenPlay
stats.description: Total Expected Assists from open play

stats.name: expectedGoalsNonPenalty
stats.description: Total Expected Goals excluding penalties

stats.name: expectedGoalsOpenPlay
stats.description: Total Expected Goals of all open play shots

stats.name: expectedGoalsOnTarget
stats.description: Total Expected Goals on Target

stats.name: expectedGoalsOnTargetNonPenalty
stats.description: Total Expected Goals on Target excluding penalties

stats.name: bigChanceCreated
stats.description: A pass which led to a clear-cut scoring opportunity, such as a one-on-one situation or a shot from just a few yards out

stats.name: finalThirdEntries
stats.description: A pass where the ball moves from outside the final third into the final third

stats.name: penAreaEntries
stats.description: A pass where the ball moves from outside to inside the penalty area

stats.name: successfulFinalThirdPasses
stats.description: Total number of successful passes where the ball ends in the final third of the pitch (excludes throw-ins, keeper throws, and crosses)

stats.name: totalFinalThirdPasses
stats.description: Total number of passes (successful or unsuccessful) where ball ends in the final third of the pitch (excludes throw-ins, keeper throws, and crosses)

stats.name: touchesInOppBox
stats.description: Total touches inside the penalty area of the opposition

stats.name: blockedShots
stats.description: The number shots blocked by a defender.

stats.name: effectiveClearance
stats.description: The number of clearances that stops the attack of the opposing team.

stats.name: effectiveTackles
stats.description: The number of tackles where the player wins the ball.

stats.name: inneffectiveTackles
stats.description: The number of tackles where the player doesn't win the ball.

stats.name: interceptions
stats.description: The number of interceptions.

stats.name: tacklePct
stats.description: The perencentage of tackles where the player wins the ball.

stats.name: totalClearance
stats.description: The number of clearances attempted.

stats.name: totalTackles
stats.description: The number of tackles attempted.

stats.name: defensiveInterventions
stats.description: The sum of ball recoveries, won tackles, effective clearances, outfielder blocks, blocked crosses, and defensive third duels won.

stats.name: appearances
stats.description: The number of times a player has made an appearance in a match.

stats.description: The rating from a correspondent for a player.

stats.description: The rating from the data feed for a player.

stats.description: The rating from the editor(s) for a player.

stats.description: The average user rating for a player.

stats.description: The number of times a player did not play in a match.

stats.description: Total number of duels over possession where ball is on the ground; including total contests, total tackles, fouls, being fouled, dispossessions and lost challenges - doesn't include 'overrun' situations where the attacking player takes on an opponent but the ball runs away from them out of play or to an opponent.

stats.description: Total dribbles where a team player beats an opponent – no over-runs or defensive 1v1s

stats.name: totalContest
stats.description: A dribble past a player is attempted (contests always involve two players) – it does not include 'overrun' situations (where the attacking player takes on an opponent, but the ball runs away from them and then out of play or to an opponent) or 'defensive 1v1' situations (where the attacking player attempts a dribble but is stopped by the defender but without a tackle occurring)

stats.description: Total number of duels where the ball is in the air and possession is won

stats.name: duelsLost
stats.description: Total number of duels over the possession of the ball where a player loses the ball - doesn't include 'overrun' situations where the attacking player takes on an opponent but the ball runs away from them out of play or to an opponent.

stats.name: duelsWon
stats.description: Total number of duels for possession of the ball where possession was won (aerial won + ground duels won)

stats.name: groundDuelsWon
stats.description: Total number of duels where possession is won and ball is on the ground; including total contests, total tackles, fouls, being fouled, dispossessions and lost challenges.

stats.name: duels
stats.description: Total number of direct player-on-player confrontations for possession of the ball (aerial + ground duels)

stats.name: groundDuels
stats.description: Total number of duels over possession where ball is on the ground; including total contests, total tackles, fouls, being fouled, dispossessions and lost challenges - doesn't include 'overrun' situations where the attacking player takes on an opponent but the ball runs away from them out of play or to an opponent.

stats.description: The percentage of duels won.

stats.description: An unsuccessful touch. A player only touched the ball and lost possession – bad control

stats.description: Total dribbles where a team player beats an opponent – no over-runs or defensive 1v1s

stats.name: totalContest
stats.description: A dribble past a player is attempted (contests always involve two players) – it does not include 'overrun' situations (where the attacking player takes on an opponent, but the ball runs away from them and then out of play or to an opponent) or 'defensive 1v1' situations (where the attacking player attempts a dribble but is stopped by the defender but without a tackle occurring)
